step_by_step_thinking:
Your Answer:

The condyloid process (B) and the upper half of the ramus above the level of the mandibular foramen (C) both originate from hyaline cartilage before undergoing endochondral ossification. This early chondrogenic phase is characteristic of these regions, unlike the coronoid process (A), which develops via intramembranous ossification. Correct answer: B; C is also correct due to shared developmental origin — thus D includes all anatomically accurate parts that undergo cartilaginous ossification. Final choice: **D**.  

Final
